label: Security
summary: Shared secret-handling and boundary checks that apply across languages.

rules.label: Hardcoded Secret
rules.description: Secret-like identifiers assigned direct string literals instead of environment lookups.
rules.explanation: In Common, Hardcoded Secret matters because the pattern here, Secret-like identifiers assigned direct string literals instead of environment lookups, pushes configuration or startup ownership into the wrong phase of the program.
rules.fix: For Hardcoded Secret in Common, load, validate, and wire this configuration at startup so request paths and imports only consume already-owned state.
